sections.title: Knowledge of the constraint provides a powerful prior for control.

blocks.text: Once the system knows the constraint, the robot can employ hybrid control. The robot can stay precise where motion is free and stay compliant where contact can guide the motion.

blocks.alt: Inferring the constraint allows application of hybrid control
blocks.caption: Inferring the constraint allows application of hybrid control
While different constraints require different hybrid control strategies, the system can automatically select the right one based on the inferred constraint type and parameters.

items: The constraint describes which directions to move precisely and which direction to allow the constraint to guide the motion.
Robustness is a byproduct of hybrid control.
Invoke precise motion along the free directions.
Invoke compliant motion along the constrained directions.
Forces are commanded only in directions against the constraint.
Inferring Geometric Constraints in Human Demonstrations for Robot Programming. Guru Subramani. PhD Dissertation, 2019.
A method for constraint inference using pose and wrench measurements. Subramani, Hagenow, Gleicher, Zinn. arXiv, 2020.
Inferring geometric constraints in human demonstrations. Subramani, Zinn, Gleicher. CoRL, 2018.
